sn22.31:1.1: At Sāvatthī. "Bhikkhus, I will teach you miseryand the root of misery. Listen to that …
sn22.31:1.2: "And what, bhikkhus, is misery? Form is misery; feeling is misery; perception is misery; volitional formations are misery; consciousness is misery. This is called misery.
sn22.31:1.3: "And what, bhikkhus, is the root of misery? It is this craving that leads to renewed existence, accompanied by delight and lust, seeking delight here and there; that is, craving for sensual pleasures, craving for existence, craving for extermination. This is called the root of misery."
